content.text:
Confirmed empirically — the model compresses poorly.

| Method | Size | Savings | Time |
|--------|-----:|--------:|-----:|
| Uncompressed | 416 MB | — | — |
| gzip | 386 MB | **7.2%** | 9.8 s |
| zstd -3 | 385 MB | **7.3%** | 0.6 s |

Roughly 7% savings — not enough to matter. Trained neural net weights are float32 values close to uniform-random over their trained range, so entropy is near-maximum and there's little structure for a compressor to exploit.

Implication for deployment: **sizing the transfer as ~420MB either way.** Gzip in transit (scp, http) won't help meaningfully. The places savings *would* exist:

- **Quantization** (int8 via ONNX Runtime's quantize_dynamic) — can cut size by ~4× (416 → ~105 MB) but changes inference semantics and accuracy. That's a model-engineering decision, not a packaging one.
- **Tauri DMG's built-in compression** (LZFSE) — same story as gzip; won't squeeze the model section hard.

So for the deploy policy tradeoff: the "separate deploy step" option (apcw-dM) keeps looking right. One-time 420MB transfer, self-updates stay small.

content: Grooming heat A9 (APCK proof-of-life); slated ₢A9AAQ neural-stanford-spike and railed it to first position. Next action is to mount ₢A9AAQ and begin the ONNX Runtime spike. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: Is there a current pace to work on the adoc treatment?

content.text:
Looking at the remaining racing paces:

| Pace | Touches `.adoc`? |
|------|:---:|
| ₢A9AAQ neural-stanford-spike | No — new doc is `APCNS-NeuralStanford.md` (markdown) |
| ₢A9AAK fixture-walkthrough-debug | No — manual verification, no doc work |
| ₢A9AAL deploy-and-real-data-test | No — deployment only |
| ₢A9AAM ssa-download-resolution | No — decision + small code change |
| ₢A9AAN entity-propagation-tier | Arguably *should*, but docket doesn't say so |

**Short answer: no pace currently targets `.adoc` work.**

**Gap worth flagging:** APCS0-SpecTop.adoc is the formal MCM concept model for the detection pipeline — it defines the vocabulary of tiers, mechanisms, and categories. ₢A9AAN adds a new `Propagated` mechanism variant to `apcrm_Mechanism`, which *is* pipeline vocabulary. The AAN docket doesn't mention updating APCS0 to reflect it. Either:

- Expand AAN's scope with an explicit "update APCS0 with Propagated mechanism quoin" deliverable, or
- Slate a new pace focused on APCS0 catch-up that sweeps in all pending vocabulary updates (mechanism additions, category changes, any tier semantics that have drifted since the spec was last touched)

The second shape is cleaner if multiple vocabulary items have accumulated. Want me to quickly audit APCS0 against the current code to see how much drift is there before we decide?

message.content: Can you remind me what APCS stands for?  For the record, the adoc is not healthy; we will have to iterate on it significantly before really comparing it to code.  Actually... Would you read it and MCM and AXLA now in light of our successful stanford spike commits recently: we probably want to start identifying important quoins for marrying that spike into the prototype well.

content.text:
**APCS = Ann's PHI Clipbuddy Spec** (non-terminal namespace for detection-pipeline concept model specs). `APCS0` = SpecTop — the top-level entry in that tree. Would grow to APCS1, APCS2… if the concept model decomposed further.

Reading the three in parallel, plus catching up on what the spike actually built.

19	:apcs_epic_html:                   <<apcs_epic_html,Epic HTML>>
20	:apcs_clinical_gate:               <<apcs_clinical_gate,Clinical Gate>>

29	:apcs_section_header:              <<apcs_section_header,Section Header>>
30	:apcs_section_header_s:            <<apcs_section_header,Section Headers>>
31	:apcs_narrative:                   <<apcs_narrative,Narrative>>
32	
33	// Detection resources — registries and dictionaries
34	:apcs_label_registry:              <<apcs_label_registry,Label Registry>>
35	:apcs_regex_table:                 <<apcs_regex_table,Regex Table>>
36	:apcs_surname_dictionary:          <<apcs_surname_dictionary,Surname Dictionary>>
37	:apcs_firstname_dictionary:        <<apcs_firstname_dictionary,Firstname Dictionary>>
38	:apcs_city_dictionary:             <<apcs_city_dictionary,City Dictionary>>
39	:apcs_blacklist:                   <<apcs_blacklist,Blacklist>>
40	:apcs_blacklist_s:                 <<apcs_blacklist,Blacklists>>
41	:apcs_english_whitelist:           <<apcs_english_whitelist,English Whitelist>>
42	:apcs_medical_whitelist:           <<apcs_medical_whitelist,Medical Whitelist>>
43	:apcs_whitelist:                   <<apcs_whitelist,Whitelist>>
44	:apcs_whitelist_s:                 <<apcs_whitelist,Whitelists>>
45	
46	// Finding anatomy
47	:apcs_finding:                     <<apcs_finding,Finding>>
48	:apcs_finding_s:                   <<apcs_finding,Findings>>
49	:apcs_severity:                    <<apcs_severity,Severity>>
50	:apcs_red:                         <<apcs_red,RED>>
51	:apcs_yellow:                      <<apcs_yellow,YELLOW>>
52	:apcs_pass:                        <<apcs_pass,PASS>>
53	:apcs_phi_category:                <<apcs_phi_category,PHI Category>>
54	:apcs_phi_category_ies:            <<apcs_phi_category,PHI Categories>>
55	
56	// Pipeline steps
57	:apcs_parse:                       <<apcs_parse,Parse>>
58	:apcs_regex_scan:                  <<apcs_regex_scan,Regex Scan>>
59	:apcs_label_scan:                  <<apcs_label_scan,Label Scan>>
60	:apcs_dictionary_scan:             <<apcs_dictionary_scan,Dictionary Scan>>
61	:apcs_merge:                       <<apcs_merge,Merge>>
62	
63	// Output projections
64	:apcs_anonymized_text:             <<apcs_anonymized_text,Anonymized Text>>

66	:apcs_elision_marker:              <<apcs_elision_marker,Elision Marker>>
67	:apcs_elision_marker_s:            <<apcs_elision_marker,Elision Markers>>
68	:apcs_placeholder:                 <<apcs_placeholder,Placeholder>>
69	:apcs_placeholder_s:               <<apcs_placeholder,Placeholders>>
70	
71	// end::mapping-section[]
72	
73	
74	== Overview
75	
76	This document defines the formal vocabulary for the detection pipeline in Ann's PHI Clipbuddy.
77	It specifies the concrete entities the algorithm operates on, the resources it consults, and the steps it performs.
78	
79	The application specification (APCAS) owns the user experience.
80	The prototype specification (APCPS) owns implementation details, tech stack, and data sources.
81	This document owns the detection algorithm's conceptual structure — the terms that must be precise for the pipeline to be reasoned about correctly.
82	
83	
84	== Input
85	
86	[[apcs_clipboard_content]]
87	{apcs_clipboard_content}::
88	Raw content read from the system clipboard on window focus.
89	May be HTML or plain text.
90	Consumed once, then the system clipboard is cleared.
91	
92	[[apcs_epic_html]]
93	{apcs_epic_html}::
94	HTML produced by Epic's "Copy All" function.
95	Semi-structured: bold labels for demographics, section headers for clinical categories, narrative freetext for clinical prose, signature blocks for provider attribution.
96	The structural formatting is the primary advantage over plain text — labels anchor PHI detection with high precision.
97	
98	[[apcs_clinical_gate]]
99	{apcs_clinical_gate}::
100	A heuristic that decides whether
101	{apcs_clipboard_content}
102	is clinical.
103	Scans for known label patterns (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.).
104	Acceptance threshold: two or more recognized labels present.
105	Content that fails the
106	{apcs_clinical_gate}
107	is not processed; the triage view clears.
108	
109	
110	== Parsed Structure
111	
112	[[apcs_document]]
113	{apcs_document}::
114	The result of parsing
115	{apcs_epic_html}.
116	Contains a sequence of typed
117	{apcs_span_s}
118	and the derived
119	{apcs_plain_text}.
120	
121	[[apcs_plain_text]]
122	{apcs_plain_text}::
123	A single string derived from the
124	{apcs_document}
125	by stripping HTML markup, normalizing whitespace, and concatenating span contributions with structural line breaks.
126	All detection scans operate against
127	{apcs_plain_text}.
128	Offsets in
129	{apcs_finding_s}
130	are byte positions within this string.
131	
132	[[apcs_span]]
133	{apcs_span}::
134	A typed region within the
135	{apcs_document}.
136	Each
137	{apcs_span}
138	has a kind, an optional label, text content, and a byte offset into
139	{apcs_plain_text}.
140	Three kinds:
141	{apcs_labeled_field},
142	{apcs_section_header},
143	{apcs_narrative}.
144	
145	[[apcs_labeled_field]]
146	{apcs_labeled_field}::
147	A
148	{apcs_span}
149	produced by a bold label followed by a value (e.g., `Patient: Margaret J. Thornton`).
150	The label is the bold text before the colon.
151	The value is the text after the colon, extending to the next structural boundary (another label, line break, or HTML element boundary).
152	
153	[[apcs_section_header]]
154	{apcs_section_header}::
155	A
156	{apcs_span}
157	produced by a bold label matching a known clinical section name (Chief Complaint, History of Present Illness, Assessment/Plan, etc.).
158	Contributes the header text to
159	{apcs_plain_text}
160	but carries no value — the section body follows as subsequent
161	{apcs_span_s}.
162	
163	[[apcs_narrative]]
164	{apcs_narrative}::
165	A
166	{apcs_span}
167	containing freetext not attached to a label.
168	Clinical prose, family history details, plan items.
169	PHI in
170	{apcs_narrative}
171	{apcs_span_s}
172	lacks positional anchoring and must be detected by the
173	{apcs_regex_scan}
174	or
175	{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
176	
177	
178	== Detection Resources
179	
180	=== Registries
181	
182	[[apcs_label_registry]]
183	{apcs_label_registry}::
184	The enumerated set of
185	{apcs_labeled_field}
186	labels that trigger per-word PHI extraction in the
187	{apcs_label_scan}.
188	Each entry specifies the label text (case-insensitive match) and the
189	{apcs_phi_category}
190	assigned to extracted words.
191	+
192	[cols="2,1"]
193	|===
194	| Label | Category
195	
196	| Patient:           | NAME
197	| Attending:         | PROVIDER
198	| Provider:          | PROVIDER
199	| Referring:         | PROVIDER
200	| Facility:          | FACILITY
201	| Electronically signed by: | PROVIDER
202	| Emergency Contact  | NAME
203	| Primary Care Physician | PROVIDER
204	|===
205	
206	[[apcs_regex_table]]
207	{apcs_regex_table}::
208	The enumerated set of regular expression patterns that detect structurally identifiable PHI in the
209	{apcs_regex_scan}.
210	Each entry specifies a pattern, a
211	{apcs_phi_category},
212	and a capture group index.
213	All matches produce
214	{apcs_red}
215	{apcs_finding_s}.
216	See APCPS for the full pattern table.
217	
218	=== Dictionaries
219	
220	[[apcs_blacklist]]
221	{apcs_blacklist}::
222	A word list whose members are flagged as potential PHI during the
223	{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
224	Three named
225	{apcs_blacklist_s}
226	are combined into a single lookup automaton:
227	
228	[[apcs_surname_dictionary]]
229	{apcs_surname_dictionary}::
230	US Census 2010 surnames, top 1,000 by frequency.
231	Source: public domain.
232	
233	[[apcs_firstname_dictionary]]
234	{apcs_firstname_dictionary}::
235	SSA baby names, top 1,000 by aggregate frequency.
236	Source: public domain.
237	
238	[[apcs_city_dictionary]]
239	{apcs_city_dictionary}::
240	US state capitals plus top 100 cities by population.
241	Source: public domain.
242	
243	[[apcs_whitelist]]
244	{apcs_whitelist}::
245	A word list whose members are common English or medical terms.
246	Membership in a
247	{apcs_whitelist}
248	does not prevent a
249	{apcs_finding} —
250	a word in both a
251	{apcs_blacklist}
252	and a
253	{apcs_whitelist}
254	is classified
255	{apcs_yellow}.
256	Two named
257	{apcs_whitelist_s}:
258	
259	[[apcs_english_whitelist]]
260	{apcs_english_whitelist}::
261	Top 5,000 common English words (Norvig/Google n-grams).
262	Source: public domain.
263	
264	[[apcs_medical_whitelist]]
265	{apcs_medical_whitelist}::
266	Hand-curated medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations.
267	Source: original curation.
268	
269	
270	== Finding Anatomy
271	
272	[[apcs_finding]]
273	{apcs_finding}::
274	A classified region of
275	{apcs_plain_text}.
276	Produced by one of the three detection scans.
277	Each
278	{apcs_finding}
279	carries: the matched text, a byte offset and length in
280	{apcs_plain_text},
281	a
282	{apcs_severity},
283	a
284	{apcs_phi_category},
285	and a replacement string for anonymization.
286	
287	[[apcs_severity]]
288	{apcs_severity}::
289	The confidence level of a
290	{apcs_finding}.
291	Determines default elision behavior and visual presentation.
292	Three levels:
293	
294	[[apcs_red]]
295	{apcs_red}::
296	Definite PHI.
297	Produced by the
298	{apcs_regex_scan}
299	(structural match) or the
300	{apcs_label_scan}
301	(positional authority).
302	Default: elide.
303	
304	[[apcs_yellow]]
305	{apcs_yellow}::
306	Possible PHI.
307	Produced by the
308	{apcs_dictionary_scan}
309	when a word appears in a
310	{apcs_blacklist}.
311	Default: elide.
312	
313	[[apcs_pass]]
314	{apcs_pass}::
315	Not PHI.
316	A word appearing only in a
317	{apcs_whitelist}
318	and not in any
319	{apcs_blacklist}.
320	No
321	{apcs_finding}
322	is emitted for
323	{apcs_pass}
324	words.
325	
326	[[apcs_phi_category]]
327	{apcs_phi_category}::
328	The HIPAA Safe Harbor category of a
329	{apcs_finding}.
330	Determines the
331	{apcs_placeholder}
332	text used in
333	{apcs_anonymized_text}.
334	Enumerated: NAME, PROVIDER, FACILITY, DATE, DOB, PHONE, EMAIL, SSN, MRN, ACCOUNT, ENCOUNTER_ID, ADDRESS, HEALTH_PLAN_ID, DEVICE_ID, NPI, DEA, URL, IP_ADDRESS.
335	
336	
337	== Pipeline Steps
338	
339	The detection pipeline executes five steps in fixed order.
340	Each step either transforms input or produces
341	{apcs_finding_s}.
342	
343	[[apcs_parse]]
344	{apcs_parse}::
345	Transform
346	{apcs_epic_html}
347	into a
348	{apcs_document}:
349	a sequence of typed
350	{apcs_span_s}
351	and derived
352	{apcs_plain_text}.
353	HTML structure is consumed; output is position-mapped text.
354	
355	[[apcs_regex_scan]]
356	{apcs_regex_scan}::
357	Scan
358	{apcs_plain_text}
359	against every pattern in the
360	{apcs_regex_table}.
361	Each match emits a
362	{apcs_red}
363	{apcs_finding}
364	with the
365	{apcs_phi_category}
366	specified by the pattern.
367	
368	[[apcs_label_scan]]
369	{apcs_label_scan}::
370	For each
371	{apcs_labeled_field}
372	whose label appears in the
373	{apcs_label_registry}:
374	extract the value text, segment into words, and emit one
375	{apcs_red}
376	{apcs_finding}
377	per word with the
378	{apcs_phi_category}
379	specified by the registry entry.
380	
381	[[apcs_dictionary_scan]]
382	{apcs_dictionary_scan}::
383	Scan
384	{apcs_plain_text}
385	against the combined
386	{apcs_blacklist}
387	automaton.
388	For each hit, check
389	{apcs_whitelist}
390	membership.
391	Emit a
392	{apcs_yellow}
393	{apcs_finding}
394	if the word is in any
395	{apcs_blacklist}
396	(regardless of
397	{apcs_whitelist}
398	membership).
399	Words in a
400	{apcs_whitelist}
401	only receive no
402	{apcs_finding}.
403	
404	[[apcs_merge]]
405	{apcs_merge}::
406	Combine all
407	{apcs_finding_s}
408	from the three scans.
409	When multiple
410	{apcs_finding_s}
411	occupy the same byte range, retain only the one with highest
412	{apcs_severity}
413	({apcs_red} > {apcs_yellow} > {apcs_pass}).
414	
415	
416	== Output Projections
417	
418	[[apcs_anonymized_text]]
419	{apcs_anonymized_text}::
420	{apcs_plain_text}
421	with elided
422	{apcs_finding_s}
423	replaced by their
424	{apcs_placeholder_s}.
425	Findings toggled to
426	{apcs_pass}
427	by the clinician pass through with original text.
428	The output format for pasting into Open Evidence.
429	
430	[[apcs_placeholder]]
431	{apcs_placeholder}::
432	A category-specific substitution string used in
433	{apcs_anonymized_text}.
434	Format: `[CATEGORY]` (e.g., `[NAME]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`).
435	Exception: DOB emits `Age: NN` (computed from date of birth and current date; ages over 89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor).
436	
437	[[apcs_assay]]
438	{apcs_assay}::
439	{apcs_plain_text}
440	with
441	{apcs_finding_s}
442	annotated inline using
443	{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
444	A diagnostic projection — shows the detection decisions within the document for review.
445	Not user-facing; used for pipeline quality assessment.
446	
447	[[apcs_elision_marker]]
448	{apcs_elision_marker}::
449	The annotation unit in an
450	{apcs_assay}.
451	Format: `‹SEVERITY:CATEGORY matched text›` (guillemet-delimited).
452	Shows the
453	{apcs_severity},
454	{apcs_phi_category},
455	and original text of each
456	{apcs_finding}
457	inline with surrounding
458	{apcs_plain_text}.
459

== Overview

This document defines the formal vocabulary for the detection pipeline in Ann's PHI Clipbuddy.
It specifies the concrete entities the algorithm operates on, the resources it consults, and the steps it performs.

The application specification (APCAS) owns the user experience.
The prototype specification (APCPS) owns implementation details, tech stack, and data sources.
This document owns the detection algorithm's conceptual structure — the terms that must be precise for the pipeline to be reasoned about correctly.


== Input

[[apcs_clipboard_content]]
{apcs_clipboard_content}::
Raw content read from the system clipboard on window focus.
May be HTML or plain text.
Consumed once, then the system clipboard is cleared.

[[apcs_epic_html]]
{apcs_epic_html}::
HTML produced by Epic's "Copy All" function.
Semi-structured: bold labels for demographics, section headers for clinical categories, narrative freetext for clinical prose, signature blocks for provider attribution.
The structural formatting is the primary advantage over plain text — labels anchor PHI detection with high precision.

[[apcs_clinical_gate]]
{apcs_clinical_gate}::
A heuristic that decides whether
{apcs_clipboard_content}
is clinical.
Scans for known label patterns (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.).
Acceptance threshold: two or more recognized labels present.
Content that fails the
{apcs_clinical_gate}
is not processed; the triage view clears.


== Parsed Structure

[[apcs_document]]
{apcs_document}::
The result of parsing
{apcs_epic_html}.
Contains a sequence of typed
{apcs_span_s}
and the derived
{apcs_plain_text}.

[[apcs_plain_text]]
{apcs_plain_text}::
A single string derived from the
{apcs_document}
by stripping HTML markup, normalizing whitespace, and concatenating span contributions with structural line breaks.
All detection scans operate against
{apcs_plain_text}.
Offsets in
{apcs_finding_s}
are byte positions within this string.

[[apcs_span]]
{apcs_span}::
A typed region within the
{apcs_document}.
Each
{apcs_span}
has a kind, an optional label, text content, and a byte offset into
{apcs_plain_text}.
Three kinds:
{apcs_labeled_field},
{apcs_section_header},
{apcs_narrative}.

[[apcs_labeled_field]]
{apcs_labeled_field}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label followed by a value (e.g., `Patient: Margaret J. Thornton`).
The label is the bold text before the colon.
The value is the text after the colon, extending to the next structural boundary (another label, line break, or HTML element boundary).

[[apcs_section_header]]
{apcs_section_header}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label matching a known clinical section name (Chief Complaint, History of Present Illness, Assessment/Plan, etc.).
Contributes the header text to
{apcs_plain_text}
but carries no value — the section body follows as subsequent
{apcs_span_s}.

[[apcs_narrative]]
{apcs_narrative}::
A
{apcs_span}
containing freetext not attached to a label.
Clinical prose, family history details, plan items.
PHI in
{apcs_narrative}
{apcs_span_s}
lacks positional anchoring and must be detected by the
{apcs_regex_scan}
or
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.


== Detection Resources

=== Registries

[[apcs_label_registry]]
{apcs_label_registry}::
The enumerated set of
{apcs_labeled_field}
labels that trigger per-word PHI extraction in the
{apcs_label_scan}.
Each entry specifies the label text (case-insensitive match) and the
{apcs_phi_category}
assigned to extracted words.
+
[cols="2,1"]
|===
| Label | Category

| Patient:           | NAME
| Attending:         | PROVIDER
| Provider:          | PROVIDER
| Referring:         | PROVIDER
| Facility:          | FACILITY
| Electronically signed by: | PROVIDER
| Emergency Contact  | NAME
| Primary Care Physician | PROVIDER
|===

[[apcs_regex_table]]
{apcs_regex_table}::
The enumerated set of regular expression patterns that detect structurally identifiable PHI in the
{apcs_regex_scan}.
Each entry specifies a pattern, a
{apcs_phi_category},
and a capture group index.
All matches produce
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding_s}.
See APCPS for the full pattern table.

=== Dictionaries

[[apcs_blacklist]]
{apcs_blacklist}::
A word list whose members are flagged as potential PHI during the
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
Three named
{apcs_blacklist_s}
are combined into a single lookup automaton:

[[apcs_surname_dictionary]]
{apcs_surname_dictionary}::
US Census 2010 surnames, top 1,000 by frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_firstname_dictionary]]
{apcs_firstname_dictionary}::
SSA baby names, top 1,000 by aggregate frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_city_dictionary]]
{apcs_city_dictionary}::
US state capitals plus top 100 cities by population.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_whitelist]]
{apcs_whitelist}::
A word list whose members are common English or medical terms.
Membership in a
{apcs_whitelist}
does not prevent a
{apcs_finding} —
a word in both a
{apcs_blacklist}
and a
{apcs_whitelist}
is classified
{apcs_yellow}.
Two named
{apcs_whitelist_s}:

[[apcs_english_whitelist]]
{apcs_english_whitelist}::
Top 5,000 common English words (Norvig/Google n-grams).
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_medical_whitelist]]
{apcs_medical_whitelist}::
Hand-curated medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations.
Source: original curation.


== Finding Anatomy

[[apcs_finding]]
{apcs_finding}::
A classified region of
{apcs_plain_text}.
Produced by one of the three detection scans.
Each
{apcs_finding}
carries: the matched text, a byte offset and length in
{apcs_plain_text},
a
{apcs_severity},
a
{apcs_phi_category},
and a replacement string for anonymization.

[[apcs_severity]]
{apcs_severity}::
The confidence level of a
{apcs_finding}.
Determines default elision behavior and visual presentation.
Three levels:

[[apcs_red]]
{apcs_red}::
Definite PHI.
Produced by the
{apcs_regex_scan}
(structural match) or the
{apcs_label_scan}
(positional authority).
Default: elide.

[[apcs_yellow]]
{apcs_yellow}::
Possible PHI.
Produced by the
{apcs_dictionary_scan}
when a word appears in a
{apcs_blacklist}.
Default: elide.

[[apcs_pass]]
{apcs_pass}::
Not PHI.
A word appearing only in a
{apcs_whitelist}
and not in any
{apcs_blacklist}.
No
{apcs_finding}
is emitted for
{apcs_pass}
words.

[[apcs_phi_category]]
{apcs_phi_category}::
The HIPAA Safe Harbor category of a
{apcs_finding}.
Determines the
{apcs_placeholder}
text used in
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Enumerated: NAME, PROVIDER, FACILITY, DATE, DOB, PHONE, EMAIL, SSN, MRN, ACCOUNT, ENCOUNTER_ID, ADDRESS, HEALTH_PLAN_ID, DEVICE_ID, NPI, DEA, URL, IP_ADDRESS.


== Pipeline Steps

The detection pipeline executes five steps in fixed order.
Each step either transforms input or produces
{apcs_finding_s}.

[[apcs_parse]]
{apcs_parse}::
Transform
{apcs_epic_html}
into a
{apcs_document}:
a sequence of typed
{apcs_span_s}
and derived
{apcs_plain_text}.
HTML structure is consumed; output is position-mapped text.

[[apcs_regex_scan]]
{apcs_regex_scan}::
Scan
{apcs_plain_text}
against every pattern in the
{apcs_regex_table}.
Each match emits a
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding}
with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the pattern.

[[apcs_label_scan]]
{apcs_label_scan}::
For each
{apcs_labeled_field}
whose label appears in the
{apcs_label_registry}:
extract the value text, segment into words, and emit one
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding}
per word with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the registry entry.

[[apcs_dictionary_scan]]
{apcs_dictionary_scan}::
Scan
{apcs_plain_text}
against the combined
{apcs_blacklist}
automaton.
For each hit, check
{apcs_whitelist}
membership.
Emit a
{apcs_yellow}
{apcs_finding}
if the word is in any
{apcs_blacklist}
(regardless of
{apcs_whitelist}
membership).
Words in a
{apcs_whitelist}
only receive no
{apcs_finding}.

[[apcs_merge]]
{apcs_merge}::
Combine all
{apcs_finding_s}
from the three scans.
When multiple
{apcs_finding_s}
occupy the same byte range, retain only the one with highest
{apcs_severity}
({apcs_red} > {apcs_yellow} > {apcs_pass}).


== Output Projections

[[apcs_anonymized_text]]
{apcs_anonymized_text}::
{apcs_plain_text}
with elided
{apcs_finding_s}
replaced by their
{apcs_placeholder_s}.
Findings toggled to
{apcs_pass}
by the clinician pass through with original text.
The output format for pasting into Open Evidence.

[[apcs_placeholder]]
{apcs_placeholder}::
A category-specific substitution string used in
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Format: `[CATEGORY]` (e.g., `[NAME]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`).
Exception: DOB emits `Age: NN` (computed from date of birth and current date; ages over 89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor).

[[apcs_assay]]
{apcs_assay}::
{apcs_plain_text}
with
{apcs_finding_s}
annotated inline using
{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
A diagnostic projection — shows the detection decisions within the document for review.
Not user-facing; used for pipeline quality assessment.

[[apcs_elision_marker]]
{apcs_elision_marker}::
The annotation unit in an
{apcs_assay}.
Format: `‹SEVERITY:CATEGORY matched text›` (guillemet-delimited).
Shows the
{apcs_severity},
{apcs_phi_category},
and original text of each
{apcs_finding}
inline with surrounding
{apcs_plain_text}.
